concerns.text: T019 specifies a 'stratified data splitter' but omits the mandatory 'stratification by solvent type' required by FR-004. This semantic weakening risks implementing standard label stratification (by diffusion value) instead of the required solvent-based stratification, violating the spec's statistical control mechanism.

T005b isolates the CPU check into a utility `device_check.py` but does not mandate its invocation at the 'startup' of the training pipeline as explicitly required by FR-003. Without an explicit integration task ensuring this check runs before model initialization, the 'at startup' constraint is silently relaxed.

T025 describes an ablation study to 'isolate graph contribution' but fails to explicitly mandate the generation of a report comparing the variation in correlation coefficients (r) between the full model and the ablated model. FR-006 requires reporting this specific variation; the task description is semantically incomplete regarding the deliverable.

T018 implements a 'Solvent-Only' baseline. While useful, FR-005 and SC-002 strictly define the primary statistical comparison as GNN vs. Linear Regression (with descriptors). Introducing a third baseline without explicit authorization in the functional requirements constitutes scope creep that dilutes the specific statistical test mandated by the spec.

T006 implements a 'fixed runtime limit' but does not specify the value (6 hours) or reference the 'free-tier CI runner' constraint from SC-003. A generic limit does not satisfy the specific success criterion of validating feasibility against the target environment's time limit.

T006b implements 'sampling or truncation logic' for high molecular weight molecules but fails to specify that this must occur 'during featurization' (before memory allocation) as required by the Edge Case in spec.md. Applying this logic too late (e.g., during training) would fail to prevent the memory crash, violating the safety constraint.

T007 relies on a hardcoded fallback URL (`zenodo.org/records/1234567...`) which appears to be a placeholder. The Constitution (Principle II) and Plan require 'Verified Accuracy' and real datasets. Implementing a task with a known fake URL violates the 'Verified Accuracy' principle before code execution begins.
